The '''Turkmenistan national football team''' ({{langx|tk|Türkmenistanyň milli futbol ýygyndysy}}) represents [[Turkmenistan]] in men's international [[association football|football]]. It is controlled by the [[Football Federation of Turkmenistan]], the governing body for football in Turkmenistan. Turkmenistan's home ground is the [[Kopetdag Stadium]] and their head coach is [[Mergen Orazow]]. The team represents [[FIFA]] and [[Asian Football Confederation]] (AFC).

==History==
After the country gained independence, they played their first match against [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] on 1 June 1992, and against fellow Central Asian nation [[Uzbekistan national football team|Uzbekistan]] on 28 June the same year.

=== 2000s ===
[[File:North Korea-Pyongyang-Kim Il-Sung Stadium-Football game with Turkmenistan-01.jpg|left|thumbnail|Turkmenistan match against [[North Korea national football team|North Korea]] in June 2008]]
They qualified for the [[2004 AFC Asian Cup]] by winning the [[2004 AFC Asian Cup qualification]], where they were placed in [[2004 AFC Asian Cup qualification#Group G 2|group G]], alongside the [[United Arab Emirates national football team|United Arab Emirates]], [[Syria national football team|Syria]] and [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]]. In the autumn of 2003, in the first leg of the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2006 FIFA World Cup qualification]], they defeated [[Afghanistan national football team|Afghanistan]] 11–0 in Ashgabat. [[Begench Kuliyev]] and [[Rejepmyrat Agabaýew]] each scored a hat-trick, while [[Guvanchmuhammet Ovekov]] scored twice. Other players on the scoresheet that day were [[Nazar Bayramov]], [[Omar Berdiyev]] and [[Didarklych Urazov]]. In the second leg the team won 0–2, with both goals scored by [[Begench Kuliyev]]. In December 2003, the national team of Turkmenistan reached the top 100 in the [[FIFA rankings]] for the first time in its history, reaching the 99th position, thanks to the successes in the 2004 AFC Asian Cup and the 2006 FIFA World Cup qualifiers. In the 2004 AFC Asian Cup that took place in China, Turkmenistan was placed in group C, with neighbours [[Uzbekistan national football team|Uzbekistan]], [[Saudi Arabia national football team|Saudi Arabia]] and [[Iraq national football team|Iraq]]. They were knocked out in the group stages following two defeats and a draw against Saudi Arabia.

=== 2010s ===

In February 2010, Turkmenistan's national football team was coach by [[Ýazguly Hojageldyýew]], who was working for [[HTTU Aşgabat]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.turkmenistan.ru/ru/node/27357|title=Главным тренером сборной Туркменистана по футболу назначен Язкули Ходжагельдыев – Интернет-газета Turkmenistan.Ru|website=www.turkmenistan.ru|access-date=23 May 2017|archive-date=3 January 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190103060047/http://www.turkmenistan.ru/ru/node/27357|url-status=live}}</ref> Under his leadership the team went to Sri Lanka to participate in the [[2010 AFC Challenge Cup]]. For the first time, they made it to the final, only to see them lose against [[North Korea national football team|North Korea]] in the penalty shootout. In the same year, the Football Association of Turkmenistan invited a native Turkmen, the head coach of [[FC Rubin Kazan]], [[Kurban Berdyev]] to resume leadership.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.tatcenter.ru/news/97695/|title=Курбан Бердыев проконсультирует туркменскую сборную|website=www.tatcenter.ru|access-date=23 May 2017|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170917032258/http://www.tatcenter.ru/news/97695/|archive-date=17 September 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.championat.com/football/news-747785.html|title=Бердыев проконсультирует сборную Туркменистана|website=www.championat.com|access-date=23 May 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160813094128/http://www.championat.com/football/news-747785.html|archive-date=13 August 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In March 2011, Turkmenistan successfully entered the final round of the [[2012 AFC Challenge Cup]], beating Pakistan, Taiwan, and played in a draw with India in the qualifying competition in Kuala Lumpur. In the summer of 2011, in the race for a spot at the [[2014 FIFA World Cup]] in Brazil, they faced [[Indonesia national football team|Indonesia]] in the second round. After managing a 1–1 draw in the first leg in [[Ashgabat|Ashghabat]], they were eventually defeated 4–3 in the second leg, 5–4 on aggregate, thus were knocked out of the contention for qualifying to the 2014 FIFA World Cup.

In winter 2012 the team gathered for a training camp in Turkey. In preparation for the [[2012 AFC Challenge Cup]], Ýazguly Hojageldyýew arranged a friendly match with Romania, which they lost 4–0.

In March 2012, the team went to Kathmandu to participate in the final tournament of the [[2012 AFC Challenge Cup]]. They beat the hosts Nepal 3–0 and the Maldives 3–1, while the final group stage match with Palestine ended in a goalless draw. In the semifinals, Turkmenistan defeated the Philippines 2–1. However, as in the previous edition, they lost the final match to North Korea with a scoreline of 1–2.

In October 2012, Turkmenistan's were invited to the [[2012 VFF Cup]] held in [[Ho Chi Minh City]] and finished as runners-up in the tournament, beating the teams of Vietnam and Laos, only to lose in the final match  against the South Korean University Selection team 0–4.

On 23 March 2013, Turkmenistan defeated [[Cambodia national football team|Cambodia]] 7–0 in Manila, in the qualifying round of the [[2014 AFC Challenge Cup]]. In the second round, Turkmenistan was to meet [[Brunei national football team|Brunei]], but the team did not arrive at the tournament, thus Turkmenistan was awarded a default 3–0 victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://tdh.gov.tm/?id=6627|title=Государственное информационное агентство Туркменистана – TDH|website=tdh.gov.tm|access-date=23 May 2017|archive-date=3 November 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131103161457/http://tdh.gov.tm/?id=6627|url-status=live}}</ref> In the last round, Turkmenistan lost to [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]] 1–0, but managed to qualify for the 2014 AFC Challenge Cup finals as the [[2014 AFC Challenge Cup qualification#Ranking of second-placed teams|best second placed team]] alongside [[Laos national football team|Laos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.the-afc.com/en/afc-challenge-2014-cup-schedule-results.html?fixtureid=7265&stageid=284&tMode=C&view=ajax&show=matchsummary|title=AFC CHALLENGE CUP 2014|website=the-afc.com|access-date=23 May 2017|archive-date=21 October 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131021203536/http://www.the-afc.com/en/afc-challenge-2014-cup-schedule-results.html?fixtureid=7265&stageid=284&tMode=C&view=ajax&show=matchsummary|url-status=live}}</ref>

In January 2014 [[Rahym Kurbanmämmedow]] was again in charge of the national team. They held three training camps in May and participated in the [[2014 AFC Challenge Cup]], where they were eliminated in the group stage, thereby losing the chance to qualify for the [[2015 AFC Asian Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.the-afc.com/afc-challenge-cup-2014-turkmenistan-philippines/afc-challenge-cup-philippines-2-0-turkmenistan|title=AFC Challenge Cup: Philippines 2–0 Turkmenistan – AFC|website=www.the-afc.com|access-date=23 May 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.the-afc.com/afc-challenge-cup-2014-afghanistan-turkmenistan/afc-challenge-cup-afghanistan-3-1-turkmenistan|title=AFC Challenge Cup: Afghanistan 3–1 Turkmenistan – AFC|website=www.the-afc.com|access-date=23 May 2017|archive-date=30 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170830141831/http://www.the-afc.com/afc-challenge-cup-2014-afghanistan-turkmenistan/afc-challenge-cup-afghanistan-3-1-turkmenistan|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.the-afc.com/afc-challenge-cup-2014-turkmenistan/afc-challenge-cup-turkmenistan-5-1-laos|title=AFC Challenge Cup: Turkmenistan 5–1 Laos – AFC|website=www.the-afc.com|access-date=23 May 2017|archive-date=30 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170830141824/http://www.the-afc.com/afc-challenge-cup-2014-turkmenistan/afc-challenge-cup-turkmenistan-5-1-laos|url-status=live}}</ref> In June 2014, as a result of their poor performance at the AFC Challenge Cup, the entire coaching staff was dismissed, including the head coach.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://turkmenportal.com/blog/4424|title=Не имеющая 8 месяцев главного тренера сборная Туркменистана скоро узнает соперников в квалификации ЧМ-2018 |website=Turkmen Portal|access-date=23 May 2017|archive-date=1 June 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160601182738/http://turkmenportal.com/blog/4424|url-status=live}}</ref>

[[File:Turkmenistan football team 2015.jpg|left|thumb|[[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2018 FIFA World Cup qualification]] match against [[Iran]] in [[Tehran]]]]

In the spring of 2015, [[Amangylyç Koçumow]] was appointed as the new head coach of the national team to prepare the team for participation in [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification]]. The team started off badly, losing to one of the weakest teams in Asia – [[Guam national football team]] 0–1. On 16 June 2015, for the first time, the Turkmenistan national team held a match outside [[Ashgabat]], at the [[Sport toplumy (Daşoguz)|Spot toplumy Stadium]] in [[Dashoguz]] in the presence of 10,000 spectators, the Turkmenistan team played a draw with Iran (1–1). Then, the team lost Oman 1:3. In the home games that took place at the [[Kopetdag Stadium]] in October 2016, Turkmenistan beat India 2–1 and Guam 1–0. In November, the Turkmenistan held a friendly match with the UAE, which ended in a 1–5 defeat and an official match with Iran, in which the team lost 1–3. On 17 November 2016, the national team of Turkmenistan in the home game sensationally beat the national team of Oman 2–1. In the final stage, the Turkmenistan national team defeated India 2–1. The Turkmenistan team took the 3rd place in Group D, which did not allow the team to go to the next stage of the qualifying games for the 2018 World Cup, but gave the opportunity to fight for getting into the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup]].

[[File:JPN-TUR 20190109 Asian Cup 3.jpg|thumb|Turkmenistan in the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup]]]]

=== Back to the AFC Asian Cup ===
Turkmenistan had finally qualified to the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup]] for the second time in history, having finished second after [[Bahrain national football team|Bahrain]]. In the tournament, Turkmenistan was grouped with [[Japan national football team|Japan]], neighbor [[Uzbekistan national football team|Uzbekistan]] and [[Oman national football team|Oman]]. The Turkmen side played pretty well in their first match against Asian powerhouse Japan and even took a lead until the end of the first half, but eventually fell to Japan 2–3. The next match against Uzbekistan was a complete disaster when Turkmenistan decided to play duel with more experienced Uzbekistan, leaving the team defeated 0–4, all four goals were scored in the first half. Turkmenistan then tried to make a fight against Oman, but lost 1–3 with two Omani goals scored in final minutes, thus the team was eliminated without scoring any point. Coach [[Ýazguly Hojageldyýew]] resigned in the aftermath of the tournament.

In March 2019,  the [[Football Federation of Turkmenistan]] named Croatian [[Ante Miše]] as the head coach of the Turkmenistan, signing a one-year contract.<ref>[https://turkmenportal.com/blog/18194/ante-mishe-ya-ne-garri-potter-chtoby-bornaya-turkmenistana-srazu-zaigrala-na-vysokom-urovne Анте Мише: «Я не Гарри Поттер, чтобы сборная Туркменистана сразу заиграла на высоком уровне»] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190331163315/https://turkmenportal.com/blog/18194/ante-mishe-ya-ne-garri-potter-chtoby-bornaya-turkmenistana-srazu-zaigrala-na-vysokom-urovne |date=31 March 2019 }} at turkmenportal.com, 28 March 2019</ref> Another croatian specialist, [[Sandro Tomić]] will help to train the national team of Turkmenistan. Croatian coaches are set to develop the overall [[football in Turkmenistan]], not just the national team. His first game, 3 month later, was a 0–0 draw at friendly match with [[Uganda]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://turkmenportal.com/blog/19596/cbornye-turkmenistana-i-ugandy-sygrali-vnichyu-v-tovarishcheskimi-matche|title=Сборные Туркменистана и Уганды сыграли вничью в товарищеском матче &#124; Спорт|website=Туркменистан, интернет портал о культурной, деловой и развлекательной жизни в Туркменистане|access-date=2 September 2019|archive-date=2 September 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190902113624/https://turkmenportal.com/blog/19596/cbornye-turkmenistana-i-ugandy-sygrali-vnichyu-v-tovarishcheskimi-matche|url-status=live}}</ref>

In the [[2023 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round|2023 AFC Asian Cup qualification]], Turkmenistan was then grouped with host [[Malaysia national football team|Malaysia]], [[Bahrain national football team|Bahrain]] and [[Bangladesh national football team|Bangladesh]]. They would go on to lose against Malaysia (1–3) and Bahrain (1–0), and despite winning against Bangladesh (1–2), Turkmenistan did not manage to qualify to the [[2023 AFC Asian Cup]]. In 2023, Turkmenistan participated in the inaugural [[2023 CAFA Nations Cup]] held in Kyrgyzstan and Uzbekistan.

In November, they lost to [[Uzbekistan national football team|Uzbekistan]] 1–3 and drew 2–2 against [[Hong Kong national football team|Hong Kong]] during the [[2026 FIFA World Cup]] and [[2027 AFC Asian Cup]] qualification.

==Team image==

[[File:TUR-UZB 20190113 Asian Cup 10.jpg|thumb|200px|The national football team of Turkmenistan in the green home kit]]

|}

===Home stadium===
[[File:Saparmurat Turkmenbashi Olympic Stadium.jpg|thumb|[[Ashgabat Olympic Stadium]]|263x263px]]
From the moment of its formation (1992) until now, the main home stadium of the Turkmenistan national football team is the [[Köpetdag Stadium]] in [[Ashgabat]], built and opened in 1997. This stadium is also the venue for home matches of [[FK Köpetdag Aşgabat]]. It was reconstructed in 2015 and currently holds 26,503 spectators.

From 2003 until the end of 2012, the main home stadium of the Turkmenistan national team was the [[Ashgabat Olympic Stadium]] (until 2017 was named Olympic Stadium named after Saparmurat Turkmenbashi the Great), renovated 2013–2017 and accommodating 45,000 spectators. At the moment, the national team is not used for football matches.[unclear]

The national team of Turkmenistan in different years held their home matches also in other cities and stadiums of Turkmenistan. So, at the [[Nisa-Çandybil Stadium|Nisa Stadium]] in Ashgabat (matches vs China,<ref>{{cite web | title=Match Report of Turkmenistan vs China PR - 1997-05-04 - WC Qualification - Global Sports Archive | url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/1997-05-04/turkmenistan-vs-china-pr/732244/ }}</ref> Vietnam<ref>{{cite web | title=Match Report of Turkmenistan vs Vietnam - 1997-05-11 - WC Qualification - Global Sports Archive | url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/1997-05-11/turkmenistan-vs-vietnam/732246/ }}</ref> and Tajikistan<ref>{{cite web | title=Match Report of Turkmenistan vs Tajikistan - 1997-05-25 - WC Qualification - Global Sports Archive | url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/1997-05-25/turkmenistan-vs-tajikistan/732248/ }}</ref> in May 1997), at the [[Sport toplumy (Daşoguz)|Sport Toplumy]] in [[Dashoguz]] (Match vs Iran in 2015 and vs Bahrain in 2017<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://turkmenportal.com/blog/11000/turkmenskie-futbolisty-proigrali-komande-bahreina-v-domashnem-otborochnom-matche-kubka-azii2019|title=Туркменские футболисты проиграли команде Бахрейна в домашнем отборочном матче Кубка Азии-2019 &#124; Спорт|website=Туркменистан, интернет портал о культурной, деловой и развлекательной жизни в Туркменистане|access-date=6 September 2019|archive-date=6 September 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190906134723/https://turkmenportal.com/blog/11000/turkmenskie-futbolisty-proigrali-komande-bahreina-v-domashnem-otborochnom-matche-kubka-azii2019|url-status=live}}</ref>), at the [[Sport toplumy (Balkanabat)|Sport Toplumy]] in [[Balkanabat]] (Match vs Taipei in 2017<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://turkmenportal.com/blog/12520/gari-uait-futbolisty-taivanya-priehali-v-turkmenistan-za-pobedoi|title=Гари Уайт: "Футболисты Тайваня приехали в Туркменистан за победой" &#124; Спорт|website=Туркменистан, интернет портал о культурной, деловой и развлекательной жизни в Туркменистане|access-date=6 September 2019|archive-date=6 September 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190906134353/https://turkmenportal.com/blog/12520/gari-uait-futbolisty-taivanya-priehali-v-turkmenistan-za-pobedoi|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://turkmenportal.com/blog/12522/futbolisty-sbornoi-taivanya-oprobovali-pole-stadiona-v-balkanabate|title=Футболисты сборной Тайваня опробовали поле стадиона в Балканабате &#124; Спорт|website=Туркменистан, интернет портал о культурной, деловой и развлекательной жизни в Туркменистане|access-date=6 September 2019|archive-date=6 September 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190906134352/https://turkmenportal.com/blog/12522/futbolisty-sbornoi-taivanya-oprobovali-pole-stadiona-v-balkanabate|url-status=live}}</ref>).

! colspan="5" style="background: #DB2017; color: #FFFF00;" |Turkmenistan national football team home stadiums
|-
!Image
!Stadium
!Capacity
!Location
!Last match
|-
|-
|[[File:Ashgabat Olympic Stadium (1) (32291465498).jpg|frameless|171x171px]]
| style="text-align:left" |[[Saparmurat Turkmenbashy Olympic Stadium|Ashgabat Olympic Stadium]]
| style="text-align:left" |45,000
| style="text-align:left" |[[Ashgabat]]
| style="text-align:left" |'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|IDN}}<small> (23 July 2011;  [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)]])</small> <ref>{{cite web | title=Turkmenistan vs Indonesia - July 23, 2011 - WC Qualification 2014 Brazil - Soccer &#124; Global Sports Archive | url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/2011-07-23/turkmenistan-vs-indonesia/407397/ }}</ref>
|-
|
| style="text-align:left" |[[Köpetdag Stadium]]
| style="text-align:left" |	26,503
| style="text-align:left" |[[Ashgabat]]
| style="text-align:left" |'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|SRI}}<small> (19 November 2019;  [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)]] )</small><ref>{{cite web | title=Turkmenistan vs Sri Lanka - November 19, 2019 - WC Qualification 2022 Qatar - Soccer &#124; Global Sports Archive | url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/2019-11-19/turkmenistan-vs-sri-lanka/1729696/ }}</ref>
|-
|[[File:Ashgabat suburbs IMG 5855 (26018615962).jpg|frameless|171x171px]]
| style="text-align:left" |[[Ashgabat Stadium]]
| style="text-align:left" |20,000
| style="text-align:left" |[[Ashgabat]]
| style="text-align:left" |'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|THA}}<small> (10 June 2025; [[2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round]])</small> <ref>{{cite web | title=Turkmenistan vs Thailand - June 10, 2025 - AFC Asian Cup Qualification 2027 Saudi Arabia - Soccer &#124; Global Sports Archive | url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/2025-06-10/turkmenistan-vs-thailand/3587590/ }}</ref>
|-
|
| style="text-align:left" |[[Sport toplumy (Daşoguz)|Dashoguz Sport Toplumy]]
| style="text-align:left" |10,000
| style="text-align:left" |[[Daşoguz]]
| style="text-align:left" |'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|BHR}}<small> (13 June 2017; [[2019 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round]])</small> <ref>{{cite web | title=Turkmenistan vs Bahrain - June 13, 2017 - AFC Asian Cup Qualification 2019 UAE - Soccer &#124; Global Sports Archive | url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/2017-06-13/turkmenistan-vs-bahrain/760304/ }}</ref>
|-
|
| style="text-align:left" |[[Sport toplumy (Balkanabat)|Balkanabat Sport Toplumy]]
| style="text-align:left" |10,000
| style="text-align:left" |[[Balkanabat]]

|-
|
| style="text-align:left" |[[Nusaý Stadium]]
| style="text-align:left" |	3,000
| style="text-align:left" |[[Ashgabat]]
| style="text-align:left" |'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|TJK}}<small> (25 May 1997; [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)]])</small> <ref>{{cite web | title=Match Report of Turkmenistan vs Tajikistan - 1997-05-25 - WC Qualification - Global Sports Archive | url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/1997-05-25/turkmenistan-vs-tajikistan/732248/ }}</ref>
|}

===Rivalries===
[[File:TUR-UZB 20190113 Asian Cup 12.jpg|thumb|right|280px|Match with Uzbekistan team in the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup|2019 Asian Cup in UAE]]]]
The main rivals of the Turkmenistan national team are the countries of [[Central Asia]], the national teams of [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]], [[Tajikistan national football team|Tajikistan]], [[Uzbekistan national football team|Uzbekistan]] and [[Kyrgyzstan national football team|Kyrgyzstan]]. The main and most important rival of the national team of Turkmenistan are the national teams of Uzbekistan and Tajikistan. The matches between the countries of Central Asia have always aroused great interest among fans of Central Asia. Football is one of the instruments of rivalry between the states of Central Asia.{{citation needed|date=July 2020}}

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Turkmenistan national football team results}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

===Coaching history===

{{Div col|colwidth=22em}}
* {{Flagicon|TKM|1992}} [[Baýram Durdyýew]] (1992–1996)
* {{Flagicon|GEO|1990}} [[Elguja Gugushvili]] (1996–1997)
* {{Flagicon|TKM|1997}} [[Täçmyrat Agamyradow]] (1997–1998)
* {{Flagicon|UKR|1992}} [[Viktor Pozhechevskyi]] (1998–1999)
* {{Flagicon|Russia}} {{Flagicon|TKM|1997}} [[Kurban Berdyev|Gurban Berdyýew]] (1999)
* {{Flagicon|TKM|1997}} [[Röwşen Muhadow]] (1999–2000)
* {{Flagicon|TKM|1997}} [[Täçmyrat Agamyradow]] (2000–2001)
* {{Flagicon|UKR|1992}} [[Volodymyr Bezsonov]] (2002–2003)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Rahym Gurbanmämmedow]] (2003–2004)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Boris Grigorýanc]] (2005)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Amangylyç Goçumow]] (2005–2006)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Rahym Gurbanmämmedow]] (2007–2009)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Boris Grigorýanc]] (2009–2010)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Ýazguly Hojageldyýew]] (2010–2014)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Rahym Gurbanmämmedow]] (2014)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Amangylyç Koçumow]] (2015–2016)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Ýazguly Hojageldyýew]] (2017–2019)
* {{Flagicon|CRO}} [[Ante Miše]] (2019–2020)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Röwşen Muhadow]] (2021)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Ýazguly Hojageldyýew]] (2021)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Said Seýidow]] (2022)<ref>{{cite web | url=http://tff.com.tm/ru/news-parent/said-seyidow-turkmenistanyn-milli-yygyndysynyn-bas-talimcisi-wezipesine-bellenildi | title=Федерация Футбола Туркменистана | access-date=11 May 2022 | archive-date=3 April 2022 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220403152929/http://tff.com.tm/ru/news-parent/said-seyidow-turkmenistanyn-milli-yygyndysynyn-bas-talimcisi-wezipesine-bellenildi | url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url=https://turkmenportal.com/blog/45716/ | title=Саид Сеидов назначен главным тренером сборной Туркменистана по футболу &#124; Спорт | access-date=11 May 2022 | archive-date=11 May 2022 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220511183812/https://turkmenportal.com/blog/45716/ | url-status=live }}</ref>
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Ýazguly Hojageldyýew]] (2022)
* {{Flagicon|TKM}} [[Mergen Orazow]] (2023–2025)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://tff.com.tm/ru/news-parent/tff-nin-yerine-yetiriji-komitetinin-nobatdaky-yygnagy-gecirildi |title=Состоялось очередное заседание исполкома Федерации футбола Туркменистана |access-date=13 January 2023 |archive-date=11 January 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230111135212/http://tff.com.tm/ru/news-parent/tff-nin-yerine-yetiriji-komitetinin-nobatdaky-yygnagy-gecirildi |url-status=live }}</ref>
* {{flagicon|TKM}} [[Rovshen Meredov]] (2025–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following 23 players were named in the squad for the [[Exhibition game|friendlies]] & [[2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification]] against [[Laos national football team|Laos]] and [[Thailand national football team|Thailand]] on 26–31 Match 2026.

''Caps and goals correct as of 18 November 2025, after the match against [[Chinese Taipei national football team|Chinese Taipei]].''

{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Withdrew due to injury
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad / standby
*<sup>RET</sup> = Retired from the national team
*<sup>SUS</sup> = Serving suspension
*<sup>WD</sup> = Player withdrew from the squad due to non-injury issue.
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|18 November 2025.}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Turkmenistan |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/193/Turkmenistan.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Turkmenistan.''

'''Note''': as of 2002, only U23 teams are allowed to participate in the Asian Games' football tournament.

==Head-to-head record==
{{main|Turkmenistan national football team records and statistics}}
<section begin=headtohead />
The list shown below shows the Turkmenistan national football team '''all-time international record against opposing nations'''.

!Total||176||65||31||80||259||272||–13||
|}

{{note|a|a}} Turkmenistan was supposed to face Brunei in the [[2014 AFC Challenge Cup qualification|2014 AFC Challenge Cup qualifiers]] but the latter withdrew from the tournament. Turkmenistan was awarded a 3-goal victory for the supposed match.
<section end=headtohead />

==FIFA ranking history==
{{Chart|definition=Turkmenistan national football team FIFA ranking history.chart|data=Turkmenistan national football team FIFA ranking history.tab|width=500px}}

==Honours==
<!-- DO NOT ADD UNOFFICIAL OR FRIENDLY COMPETITIONS IN THE SUMMARY TABLE FOR OFFICIAL HONOURS ONLY -->

===Continental===
* '''[[AFC Challenge Cup]]'''
** {{silver2}} Runners-up (2): [[2010 AFC Challenge Cup|2010]], [[2012 AFC Challenge Cup|2012]]

===Friendly===
* '''Turkmenistan President's Cup (1)''': 1997
* '''HCM City Cup (1)''': 2008

===Summary===
Only official honours are included, according to ''FIFA statutes'' (competitions organized/recognized by [[FIFA]] or an affiliated confederation).
